segments.text: God, who in the past spoke to our forefathers through the prophets at various times and in many ways,
has in these last days spoken to us through his Son. God appointed the Son heir of everything, and made the universe through him.

The Son is the radiant glory of God, and the visible expression of his true character. He sustains everything by his powerful command. When he had provided cleansing for sin he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty in heaven.
He was placed much higher than the angels since he received a greater name than them.
God never said to any angel, {
You are my Son; today I have become your Father,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: or
I will be a Father to him, and he will be a Son to me.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes.text: + Hebrews is full of Old Testament quotations and allusions, some of which are not quoted exactly or are given in summary form. It is therefore difficult at times to identify the exact source or sources. Old Testament quotes that are relatively clear will be identified here. Note that they are taken from the Septuagint, the Greek translation of the Hebrew Scriptures. The quotations referenced in this verse appear to be: Psalms 2:7, 2 Samuel 7:14, and 1 Chronicles 17:13.

segments.text: Also, when he brought his firstborn Son into the world, he said, {
Let all God's angels worship him.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes.text: + 'Firstborn: this term is not to be taken that there was a time when Jesus did not exist; it is used to identify rank rather than chronology.

segments.text: Regarding the angels, he says, {
He makes his angels winds, and his servants flames of fire,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: but about the Son he says, {
Your throne, God, lasts forever and ever, and justice is the ruling scepter of your kingdom.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You love what is right, and hate what is lawless. That is why God, your God, has placed you above everyone else by anointing you with the oil of joy.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes.text: + The ancient practice of pouring oil over the head of a person was to indicate they were being chosen for a specific position, a high honor.

segments.text: You, Lord, laid the foundations of the earth in the beginning. The heavens are the product of your hands.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: They will come to an end, but you will continue. They will wear out like clothes do,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: and you will roll them up like a cloak. Like clothes, they will be changed, but you never change, and your life never ends.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes.text: + Literally, 'your years never fail. The quotation is from Psalms 102:25-27.

segments.text: But he never said to any angel, {
Sit at my right hand until I place your enemies in subjection under your feet.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: What are the angels? They are beings that serve, sent out to help those who will receive salvation.
